key_points: Dorm themes now restricted to OSU-only content due to state law SB1
Bike theft is rampant near campus; two stolen in two weeks reported
North campus dorms plagued by loud late-night Christian concerts
key_quotes: No chalk, no dorm themes, no personality/expression — the fact that OSU is making a blatant point to restrict these seemingly trivial forms of expression has such big implications
My furniture was vibrating earlier. It's safe to say I was extremely peeved
If you've got an E-bike you've always gotta take that thing inside overnight. They are too valuable

key_points: Columbus is widely praised as a very queer-friendly city near campus
Fraternity activities suspended after alcohol poisoning death; hazing culture flagged
Campus has strong community spirit — students help strangers dig out cars in snowstorms
key_quotes: Columbus is a very queer city, I think you'll do numbers anywhere close to campus
When will these frats 'get it'? They're killing people in the name of 'Brotherhood'
I attended the back to school bash and played Capture the Flag with over 150 people

key_points: GrubHub delivery robots are a campus novelty, spotted even off-campus
A chocolate-making class is a real and popular course option
Limited direct complaints about food quality in the data — not a major discussion topic
key_quotes: Also for the chocolate class how good does the chocolate taste that you make. Also I'm a hella picky eater how is the food for picky eaters
He was involved in the recent layoffs but still has bills to pay. He's working that corner now [about a delivery robot off-campus]

key_points: Students working full-time while taking full-time classes report feeling alone and overwhelmed
Redemption stories exist — one student failed first semester, earned doctorate nine years later
Strong peer community on Reddit provides emotional support when institutional support feels absent
key_quotes: I work full time while taking full time classes and sometimes I just feel like I'm the only one struggling
If you are struggling now, I can't promise it will get easier, but it is still possible to succeed
The long days and sacrifices will pay off. I know you see your well-off friends living it up

key_points: New free tuition for perfect SAT/ACT scorers plus $5,000 research stipend announced
Admitted students with fee waivers still hit with $100 acceptance fee before aid applies
Alumni openly refusing donations due to Wexner scandal and admin decisions
key_quotes: I got a -1500 on FAFSA and still had to pay my acceptance and housing fees before I got any of my aid applied
Are you shitting me? I had a perfect ACT and got $1000/year and nothing else. Just a few years too early I guess
Alumni donations are on indefinite hold until they stop pandering to Wexner

key_points: TBDBITL marching band demands 10+ hours weekly plus memorization time — serious commitment
Canvas (Instructure) was hacked twice, causing student data stress during finals
OSU CS ranked around #41 nationally; strong STEM reputation acknowledged broadly
key_quotes: It's really, really hard. It is a lot of dedication. You have to be at 110% every single rehearsal
Depending on your ability, memorizing the music adds 20 hours a week or more
Ohio state has some great STEM programs — that said, you have a lot of maturity for your age

key_points: FIRE gave OSU an F grade on free speech; chalk ban and protest crackdowns cited repeatedly
University president resigned over inappropriate relationship; OSU faces serial scandal problem
ACLU sued OSU after student expelled for pro-Palestinian online comments — federal judge ruled against OSU
key_quotes: Good God, can we go 1 week without some sort of OSU scandal?
It's wild how fast OSU dove into censorship after I graduated. I don't even recognize my alma mater anymore
OSU is committed to protecting the freedom of expression — by banning sidewalk chalking

key_points: Campus is visually beautiful — Thompson Library, Oval, Mirror Lake draw consistent praise
Parking is outrageously expensive; Campus Parc universally hated by students
Columbus city itself is vibrant, queer-friendly, and full of student-friendly amenities
key_quotes: I pay more per month for parking than my health AND life insurance combined. Shit is ridiculous
I found the campus lovely — the Thompson Library, Ohio Union, Orton Geological Building, and Mirror Lake were all incredible
I can smell spring in Columbus all the way from Nevada!! I miss mulch

key_points: JD Vance (OSU undergrad) cited as proof state school grads reach top positions
Fisher School of Business seen as less prestigious than Kelley at IU by students themselves
Strong alumni network but alumni increasingly withholding donations over scandal damage
key_quotes: Ohio State to VP of USA. Insert your own state school to the sentence above
Fisher doesn't have the same prestige that Kelley does. I want to know if I can be as successful coming out of Fisher
An OSU degree in the right fields are still viewed as a prestigious degree and opens so many doors

key_points: In-state students can now get free tuition with perfect test scores or via regional campus pathway
Out-of-state costs are steep; international students especially feel the financial pressure
Students openly question tuition value when admin buys military MRAPs and invites crypto speakers at graduation
key_quotes: Is this where our tuition is going? Last I checked we sorted out our IED problem last November
I want a refund on my graduation speech that was about crypto — I want a real speech from literally anyone else
Coming out of undergrad debt free will be better in the long run than the prestige of a T20

key_points: Enormous school energy — football, Brutus, Script Ohio create genuine shared identity
Politically turbulent campus with active student protest culture pushing back on admin
Warm, community-oriented student body; older students and nontraditional students feel welcomed
key_quotes: This past week was my first at OSU. I was very worried that I, being 34, would struggle to fit in — I'm so happy to report that is not the case
Somehow we've arrived at the point where the university is bringing shame to the football program and not vice versa
Most people are decent people — Made me smile. Need to see more community in these troubling times

key_points: Epstein ties: Leslie Wexner named congressional co-conspirator; his name is on major buildings including the medical center
Free speech: FIRE F-rating, chalk banned, protests broken up by police, student expelled for online comments
Serial scandals: president resigned over relationship, professor assaulted cameraman, Epstein payments to OSU gynecologist documented
key_quotes: It is an embarrassment to be associated with a school who protects and defends Wexner just because of his money
FOD has been instructed via email to remove and document the W tape overs to aid OSU PD in finding out who is doing it
Jeffrey Epstein made quarterly $25,000 payments to Ohio State head of gynecology for 'consultant work'

key_points: Orton Geological Museum mimics the geological record in its actual building materials — genuinely unique
Xerxes, a resident red-tailed hawk near Engineering, has become a beloved campus celebrity
Thompson Library 11th floor is a stunning study spot rarely mentioned on official tours
key_quotes: Orton Geological museum is the only known building that mimics the geological record with the materials used to make it
Xerxes. King of the hawks — there are some nesting by engineering
The stadium tour is a cool experience — glad you got to see some of the prettier spots on a non-game day
